summary: This is an extremely brief post where Joel shares a quote from a blogger named Geodog who attended one of Joel's meetups at a Berkeley cafe. The quote humorously describes how the blogger identified the tech gathering by spotting a table of poorly-dressed males fitting the stereotypical programmer demographic. Joel presents the quote without commentary, letting the self-deprecating humor speak for itself.
key_points: Joel hosted informal meetups with readers, reflecting his community-building approach
The quote plays on the recognizable visual stereotypes of tech gatherings — predominantly male, not fashionable, and demographically homogeneous
Joel's choice to share this without comment suggests he found the observation both funny and accurate
money_quotes: when I spotted a big table lined entirely with males, mostly in their mid-twenties to early forties, not too well dressed, predominantly European-American, I knew that I had found the geek gathering.
key_insight: The tech community's demographic homogeneity was so visually obvious in 2004 that a stranger could identify a programmer meetup at a glance.
